name: Consulting
description: Executive consulting style. Maximum clarity, data-driven, executive-ready.
chartStyle: Clean, labeled, sourced. Column/bar preferred. No 3D.
specialRules: Every slide must pass 'so what' test. Pyramid Principle structure. Source citations mandatory.

name: Corporate
description: Standard business presentations. Professional, clean, versatile.
titleStyle: Action titles or descriptive titles with bold lead-in
specialRules: Consistent headers across slides. Logo placement if provided.

name: Sales
description: Persuasive, benefit-focused, visually engaging. Designed to convince.
whitespace: Very generous — let key messages breathe
chartStyle: Bold colors, highlight the winning metric
specialRules: Lead with customer pain → solution → proof → CTA. Use big numbers for ROI/impact.

name: Startup
description: Modern, energetic, forward-looking. For pitch decks and product launches.
contentDensity: Low — lots of whitespace, one idea per slide
specialRules: Problem → Solution → Market → Traction → Team → Ask. Big numbers for TAM/growth.

name: Keynote
description: High-impact, minimal text, visual-first. For conference talks.
contentDensity: Very low — one element per slide
background: Mix of white and dark slides for rhythm
specialRules: Design for projection at distance. Minimum 20pt for any text.

name: Educational
description: Clear, structured, pedagogical. For training and workshops.
titleStyle: Learning objective or topic + key point
specialRules: Start sections with objectives. Use numbered steps. Include 'Key Takeaway' callouts.

name: Academic
description: Rigorous, citation-heavy, data-focused. For research and conferences.
chartStyle: Publication-quality, full axis labels, error bars
specialRules: Every claim needs a citation. Methodology slides required. Statistical significance noted.

name: Creative
description: Expressive, visually rich, design-forward. For agencies and design teams.
background: Varied — mix white, color, image backgrounds
specialRules: Visual storytelling over data. Large imagery. Typography as design element.
